channel.title: Comments for page "Holy Science"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Holy Science"

item.title: Holy Science/The Mission GOI
item.content:encoded: Fish Truck has added the inaugural collaboration to this. I posted before but this is now available (with one unique contributor) to become a GOI in of itself. It is similar to HI and ORIA that it is Abrahmic but now we have three of them and don't you think that's fitting. Also it's Catholic and their canon is already very aesthetic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I was definitely trying to delve into Adult Horror with this series and I hope more people do stuff with it. Each of the entries aside from 1616 explore some sort of bad thing that exists in reality. A lot of monsters in horror are just symbols for something else.

Also just a side note, religious zealotry is just incidental to this. Ezekial is a reality bender who is not aware that he's a reality bender, but reality benders are people and they grow up experiencing cultural influences. It's not intended to be a commentary or critique of religion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going double backsies on this. This is an open GOI or canon now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… The author's account is deleted and we shouldn't expect any more additions? This is a real bummer.

I'm in awe of the series. Reality benders are pretty cool but what really got me is the topics used. SCP project is described as sci-fi, urban fantasy, horror, and this particular series dips into the topics which are horrors and tragedies irl. The kind you don't just read about in the internet but probably experienced irl: religious zealocy, domestic abuse, unwanted pregancy/miscarriage.

The surreal nightmare-like writing narration/action were praised plenty before me, but I want to reiterate that there's horror in what some of the characters went through. These horrors are real and relatable, and I appreciate the "this but the victim was a reality bender and the outcome is grim".

My last two remaining braincells exhausted themselves writing this but I wanna give my praise even if it sounds rambly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to go ahead and open this up as an open group/poi/canon. I feel like there's a lot more that can expanded upon here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just letting you guys know that I added 3682 since it's out now, if I wasn't meant to do that, then please tell me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have I mentioned that I love this series? I love this series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ezekiel doesn't know that he's a reality bender. He believes all of the things that come from his subconscious suggestion are divine providence.

The keter hamster was a lab rat at Niwareka.

That being said, all of these articles are built to stand on their own without being interconnected, so author death still applies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's one heck of a hub! I was not expecting that tag at the bottom!

Will the interconnections between these articles make sense once the fifth is released? I mean, I get the last three, or at least two of them, but the Keter hamster?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: CAATHOLIC SCIENCE IS THE BEST IN THE WORLD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not ready but I'm still fucking hyped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, Faminepulse is contributing to the SCP-3000 contest. Oh nooooo… it's gonna get spooky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ayyy finally a hub for this series

maybe ill finally understand what it's about after this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a crosslink hub for a series of interconnected articles. This is not a new GOI or tale series.

This is probably also not a teaser for a contest entry.
